---
#title: "People"
# listing:
#   contents: people
#   sort: "date desc"
#   type: default
#   categories: false
#   sort-ui: false
#   filter-ui: false
page-layout: article
title-block-banner: false
toc: true
toc-title: "Members"
toc-depth: 4
---


<!-- He studied at [Peking University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peking_University), then trained at [UCLA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_California,_Los_Angeles) with Dr. Charles Taylor on language evolution and artificial life, and at the [University of Notre Dame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Notre_Dame) with Dr. [Nora Besansky](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nora_J._Besansky) on ecological genomics of adaptation and speciation. -->

<!-- As a postdoctoral fellow at the [University of Texas at Austin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Texas_at_Austin), he worked with Dr. [Mark Kirkpatrick](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_Kirkpatrick) on population genetics and theoretical biology, and with Dr. [David Houle](https://www.bio.fsu.edu/faculty.php?faculty-id=houle) on quantitative genetics. He then worked as a senior computational research scientist at [St. Jude Children's Research Hospital](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Jude_Children%27s_Research_Hospital) on pediatric cancer genomics. -->

<!-- He is a faculty member and head of bioinformatics in the Stem Cell Program and the Institute for Cancer Outcomes and Survivorship at the Heersink School of Medicine, [University of Alabama at Birmingham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Alabama_at_Birmingham_School_of_Medicine). His work applies genetics, bioinformatics, and computational biology to cancer and other complex diseases. -->

The lab works on cancer genomics and single-cell biology with computational methods.

## Current Members

### Dr. Venkata Krishna Chaithanya Manam - Postdoctoral Fellow

Dr. Manam earned his Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from [Purdue University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purdue_University). His doctoral work used natural language processing to detect and resolve ambiguities in crowdsourcing task instructions, with publications in [*IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters*]{.underline} and at the HCOMP and WWW conferences. He was an AI and NLP Research Scientist at Reveal AI before joining the lab. He works on machine-learning methods for single-cell perturbation data.

### Fadeela Sheini - Vision Science PhD Student

Fadeela Sheini, OD, is a Vision Science PhD student at UAB, co-mentored with Dr. Maria Grant. Her work covers experimental design and quantitative analysis of retinal tissue responses to metabolic and ischemic stress. She studies lipid metabolism and inflammation in diabetic retinopathy and retinopathy of prematurity, to identify strategies that preserve vascular stability and protect retinal function.


## Alumni 

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
library(knitr)

df <- data.frame(
  Name = c("Zihao Zhang","Yassin Mreyoud","Doug Welsch", "Brunno Macedo"),
  Role = c("Machine learning, 2025 Fall","Single-cell genomics, 2025 Summer","Bioinformatics, 2024-25", "CML genomics, 2024 Fall"),
  School = c("Chinese Academy of Sciences | Nutrition","WashU | Medicine","Rice | Computer Science", "UFPE, Brazil"),
  Lastknown = c("UAB","UAB | Medicine","Oklahoma Medical Research Foundation | Donczew Lab","UAB | Matlawska Lab")
)

kable(df, format = "html",
      col.names = c("Name", "Project", "From", "Current Position"),
      table.attr = "class='table table-striped'")
```


### Yassin Mreyoud - MD/PhD Candidate

He was a bioinformatics scientist at [Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Washington_University_School_of_Medicine), with several publications including one in [*BMC Bioinformatics*]{.underline}. He is now an MSTP MD/PhD student at UAB. His work covers immune dysregulation and cell-cell communication in disease, spanning multi-omics integration, communication-inference algorithms, and single-cell analysis pipelines.

<!-- --- -->

### Doug Welsch - Bioinformatician

He earned a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from [Rice University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_University), with a minor in Biochemistry and Cell Biology. As a student researcher at Rice, he worked in Dr. M. Neal [Waxham's lab](https://sites.google.com/uth.edu/waxham-lab/home) at the University of Texas Health Science Center at Houston on cryo-EM image analysis of synthetic lipid vesicles, and co-authored a paper in [*Biophysical Reports*]{.underline}. He then worked in Dr. [Hongjie Li's lab](https://www.bcm.edu/people-search/hongjie-li-66406) at Baylor College of Medicine, with Dr. Tzu-Chiao Lu, on single-cell data analysis, and co-authored a paper in [*Science*]{.underline}.

With the lab, he co-authored papers in [*Blood*]{.underline} and [*Cancer Gene Therapy*]{.underline}, among others.

### Brunno Macedo - PhD Student

He earned a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences from the [Federal University of Pernambuco](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_University_of_Pernambuco), Brazil, where he worked in clinical and laboratory hematology and served two years as a teaching assistant for undergraduate and specialization courses. He then completed a Master of Science at the [University of São Paulo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_S%C3%A3o_Paulo), Ribeirão Preto Medical School, in the Internal Medicine Graduate Program. For his Master's he worked in [Dr. Fabiola Traina's lab](https://rio.fmrp.usp.br/fabiolatraina/) on molecular alterations and signaling pathways in myeloid neoplasms. He is now a PhD student at the University of Alabama at Birmingham.
